original_hypothesis.title: Strategy Yield Sandwich Attack via Public Harvest
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: An attacker can extract accumulated strategy yield by flash-loaning tokens, depositing them into DegenBox to gain a massive share of the pool, calling the public `harvest` function to realize the yield, and immediately withdrawing their shares for a profit.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The `harvest` function in `DegenBox` is public and can be called by anyone. When a strategy generates profit, `harvest` increases `totals[token].elastic`, which instantly increases the value of all existing shares. Because DegenBox has no deposit/withdrawal fees or timelocks, an attacker can sandwich the `harvest` call: deposit huge capital -> call harvest -> withdraw. This dilutes the yield for long-term depositors and transfers it to the attacker.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Public harvest function updates totals[token].elastic without anti-sandwich protections

preconditions: A strategy is active and has accumulated unrealized yield
Flash loans are available for the underlying token
No deposit/withdrawal fees are configured on the DegenBox

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Atomic sequence of deposit -> harvest -> withdraw
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker captures (attacker_shares / total_shares) * realized_yield, which approaches 100% of the yield if the flash loan is large enough.

economic_questions: What is the maximum flash loan size available vs the current DegenBox TVL?
Are there any hidden fees in the specific tokens being used?
validation_requirements: Simulate a flash loan sandwich attack around a profitable `harvest` call
Calculate net profit after gas and flash loan fees

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_execution_cost: Gas + flash loan fee (e.g., 0.05% on Aave/Balancer)
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Almost all accumulated strategy yield since last harvest
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: High, provided yield > flash loan fee

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Requires deep flash loan liquidity for the target token
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Right before a large strategy yield is ready to be harvested
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Theft of yield from legitimate long-term depositors

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Check strategy implementations for yield realization mechanics
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify exact share-to-amount math before and after harvest

validation_result.impact_summary: Anyone can sandwich the public `harvest()` function by depositing before and withdrawing after, stealing accumulated strategy yield from long-term DegenBox depositors.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The DegenBox.harvest() function at DegenBox.sol is public with no access control. When a strategy reports profit, the function increases totals[token].elastic, which instantly increases the value of all existing shares. Combined with the absence of deposit/withdrawal fees and timelocks, an attacker can atomically: (1) deposit a large amount to acquire a dominant share position, (2) call harvest() to realize accumulated strategy yield into totals[token].elastic, (3) withdraw, converting their shares back to tokens at the now-increased elastic/base ratio. The attacker captures yield proportional to their share of the pool during the harvest, diluting the yield that should have gone to long-term depositors. Using flash loans with zero or minimal fees (e.g., Balancer), the attacker can acquire an arbitrarily large share of the pool, approaching 100% yield extraction.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The harvest function at DegenBox.sol is definitively public with no access control modifier. The deposit function has no fees, only the `allowed(from)` modifier which passes when from == msg.sender. The withdraw function similarly has no fees. The elastic increase from harvest is immediate and affects all share holders proportionally. There is no timelock, deposit lock, or any anti-sandwich mechanism.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The harvest function is public with no access control. When balanceChange > 0, it directly increases totals[token].elastic, instantly increasing the value of all shares. Any caller can trigger this yield realization at a time advantageous to them.
No deposit fees or timelocks. An attacker can deposit immediately before calling harvest, acquiring shares at the pre-harvest elastic/base ratio.
No withdrawal fees or timelocks. The attacker immediately converts shares to tokens at the post-harvest ratio, extracting the yield increase.

calling_sequence: 1. Attacker obtains tokens via external flash loan (e.g., Balancer with 0% fee)
2. Attacker calls DegenBox.deposit(token, msg.sender, msg.sender, largeAmount, 0) - gets shares at pre-harvest ratio
3. Attacker calls DegenBox.harvest(token, false, 0) - strategy.harvest() returns positive balanceChange, totals[token].elastic increases
4. Attacker calls DegenBox.withdraw(token, msg.sender, msg.sender, 0, allShares) - converts shares at post-harvest ratio
5. Attacker repays flash loan, keeping the yield difference
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: deposit() at DegenBox.sol:170 converts amount to shares via total.toBase(amount, false) at the current elastic/base ratio. harvest() at DegenBox.sol:426 increases totals[token].elastic by strategy profit (line ~441: totals[token].elastic = totalElastic.to128()). withdraw() at DegenBox.sol:228 converts shares back to amount via total.toElastic(share, false) at the now-increased ratio. The attacker's profit equals (attacker_shares / total_shares_during_harvest) * yield_amount.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No specific deployment configuration affects this vulnerability. The harvest function is inherently public in DegenBox.sol regardless of deployment parameters.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The attack is fully viable in a single transaction: (1) Flash loan tokens from an external source with 0% or low fee, (2) deposit into DegenBox to get shares, (3) call public harvest() to realize strategy yield into elastic, (4) withdraw shares at increased value, (5) repay flash loan. The attacker's profit = (attacker_shares / total_shares) * realized_yield - flash_loan_fee. With Balancer's 0% flash loans, any positive yield is extractable. The attack requires an active strategy with unrealized yield, which is the normal operating state when strategies are deployed.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH. All DegenBox depositors with active strategies are affected. The attacker captures a portion of accumulated yield proportional to their deposited share. With sufficient flash loan capital (which is freely available), the attacker can capture nearly 100% of all pending strategy yield. This represents ongoing theft of yield from legitimate depositors every time a strategy accumulates unrealized profit.
recommended_mitigations: Add access control to harvest() - restrict to owner/operators only
Implement deposit fees that are distributed to existing share holders to make sandwich attacks unprofitable
Add a time-weighted average balance mechanism so newly deposited funds don't immediately participate in yield
Implement a minimum deposit-to-withdrawal delay (timelock) to prevent atomic sandwich attacks
Add a harvest cooldown period or make harvest only callable by authorized strategies/keepers
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy DegenBox with a mock strategy that accumulates yield. 2. Have a legitimate user deposit tokens. 3. Let strategy accumulate yield (mock strategy.harvest returns positive balanceChange). 4. In a single transaction: attacker deposits large amount, calls harvest(), withdraws all. 5. Verify attacker extracted yield that should have gone to the original depositor. 6. Compare original depositor's balance before and after to confirm yield dilution.
uncertainties: The IStrategy interface's harvest function receives msg.sender - some strategy implementations might restrict callers, though this is not guaranteed by the interface
The actual accumulated yield amounts depend on specific strategy implementations not in scope
Flash loan availability and fees vary by network and protocol
additional_testing_needed: Test with realistic strategy implementations to verify harvest returns positive balanceChange when called by arbitrary addresses
Measure exact yield extraction percentage for various flash loan sizes relative to TVL
Verify attack profitability accounting for gas costs on Blast L2
Test whether BlastBox's _onBeforeDeposit check affects the attack when using BlastBox instead of raw DegenBox
